name: IEC 62443-4-2
fullName: Technical Security Requirements for IACS Components
description: Sets concrete security capabilities and rules for host, network, software application, and embedded system devices.

questions.name: IEC 62443-4-2 - Embedded User Authentication
questions.text: Are embedded devices requiring unique human user authentication (covering Siemens S7-1500 PLCs, Allen-Bradley ControlLogix, SEL RTUs, and digital relay modules)?
questions.description:
Check that PLCs, RTUs, and smart switches require individual logins for engineering modifications.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.
4. Document the Security Level achieved (SL-A) vs target requirements (SL-T) for each zone conduit.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the embedded device security configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Evaluation evidence must include: Zone and Conduit design architecture diagram, Security Level Target (SL-T) vs Security Level Achieved (SL-A) matrix, and OEM device integration manual validation registers.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Using unhardened or unpatched field controllers opens critical hardware interfaces to remote execution exploits. Attackers can leverage known vulnerabilities to flash unauthorized firmware or change safety threshold parameters on active PLCs.

questions.name: IEC 62443-4-2 - Software Process Vetting
questions.text: Are software processes authenticated before executing on embedded controllers (covering Siemens S7-1500 PLCs, Allen-Bradley ControlLogix, SEL RTUs, and digital relay modules)?
questions.description:
Verify that code signatures are checked before running ladder logic or scripts on the unit.

SOP:
1. Establish physical locking covers and secure enclosures around critical field device interfaces.
2. Configure hardware configuration locks and disable local diagnostic ports (USB, RS-232) to block local unauthorized adjustments.
3. Validate that device configuration changes require double-signature supervisor tokens before logical modifications are written to memory.
4. Document the Security Level achieved (SL-A) vs target requirements (SL-T) for each zone conduit.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the embedded device security configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Evaluation evidence must include: Zone and Conduit design architecture diagram, Security Level Target (SL-T) vs Security Level Achieved (SL-A) matrix, and OEM device integration manual validation registers.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Using unhardened or unpatched field controllers opens critical hardware interfaces to remote execution exploits. Attackers can leverage known vulnerabilities to flash unauthorized firmware or change safety threshold parameters on active PLCs.

questions.name: IEC 62443-4-2 - Local Diagnostic Lockouts
questions.text: Are physical diagnostic serial ports locked or logically disabled (covering Siemens S7-1500 PLCs, Allen-Bradley ControlLogix, SEL RTUs, and digital relay modules)?
questions.description:
Ensure RS-232, RS-485, and USB interfaces are physically locked or disabled to prevent local intrusions.

SOP:
1. Establish physical locking covers and secure enclosures around critical field device interfaces.
2. Configure hardware configuration locks and disable local diagnostic ports (USB, RS-232) to block local unauthorized adjustments.
3. Validate that device configuration changes require double-signature supervisor tokens before logical modifications are written to memory.
4. Document the Security Level achieved (SL-A) vs target requirements (SL-T) for each zone conduit.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the embedded device security configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Evaluation evidence must include: Zone and Conduit design architecture diagram, Security Level Target (SL-T) vs Security Level Achieved (SL-A) matrix, and OEM device integration manual validation registers.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Using unhardened or unpatched field controllers opens critical hardware interfaces to remote execution exploits. Attackers can leverage known vulnerabilities to flash unauthorized firmware or change safety threshold parameters on active PLCs.

4. Document the Security Level achieved (SL-A) vs target requirements (SL-T) for each zone conduit.

Inspect the embedded device security configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Evaluation evidence must include: Zone and Conduit design architecture diagram, Security Level Target (SL-T) vs Security Level Achieved (SL-A) matrix, and OEM device integration manual validation registers.

questions.name: IEC 62443-4-2 - Host Hardening Checks
questions.text: Are unnecessary services and network daemons disabled on all host systems (covering Siemens S7-1500 PLCs, Allen-Bradley ControlLogix, SEL RTUs, and digital relay modules)?
questions.description:
Validate that unused services (e.g. FTP, raw Telnet, HTTP) are disabled in the device settings.

SOP:
1. Establish physical locking covers and secure enclosures around critical field device interfaces.
2. Configure hardware configuration locks and disable local diagnostic ports (USB, RS-232) to block local unauthorized adjustments.
3. Validate that device configuration changes require double-signature supervisor tokens before logical modifications are written to memory.
4. Document the Security Level achieved (SL-A) vs target requirements (SL-T) for each zone conduit.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the host device security configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Evaluation evidence must include: Zone and Conduit design architecture diagram, Security Level Target (SL-T) vs Security Level Achieved (SL-A) matrix, and OEM device integration manual validation registers.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Using unhardened or unpatched field controllers opens critical hardware interfaces to remote execution exploits. Attackers can leverage known vulnerabilities to flash unauthorized firmware or change safety threshold parameters on active PLCs.

questions.name: IEC 62443-4-2 - Network Interface Lockdown
questions.text: Are unused physical ethernet ports on network devices logically locked (enforced by Cisco Industrial Ethernet switches, network zoning firewalls, and isolated Purdue model level boundaries)?
questions.description:
Verify physical switchports are administratively disabled and locked inside secure enclosures.

SOP:
1. Establish physical locking covers and secure enclosures around critical field device interfaces.
2. Configure hardware configuration locks and disable local diagnostic ports (USB, RS-232) to block local unauthorized adjustments.
3. Validate that device configuration changes require double-signature supervisor tokens before logical modifications are written to memory.
4. Document the Security Level achieved (SL-A) vs target requirements (SL-T) for each zone conduit.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the network device security configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Evaluation evidence must include: Zone and Conduit design architecture diagram, Security Level Target (SL-T) vs Security Level Achieved (SL-A) matrix, and OEM device integration manual validation registers.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Inadequate network segmentation allows IT-OT convergence traffic to flow unmediated across enclaves. A malware infection on the corporate LAN (like ransomware) can propagate directly to critical process control loops, halting operations.

questions.name: IEC 62443-4-2 - Software App Session Lock
questions.text: Do HMI software applications automatically log out idle sessions?
questions.description:
Verify automated session lock and termination parameters in the SCADA control software interface.

SOP:
1. Deploy endpoint protection agents configured with real-time process monitoring to block unsigned scripts and execution threats.
2. Enforce automatic session logout GPOs terminating interactive operator connections after a defined period of inactivity.
3. Configure system event log forwarding to stream all reboots, login attempts, and administrative modifications to a centralized syslog receiver.
4. Document the Security Level achieved (SL-A) vs target requirements (SL-T) for each zone conduit.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the software application security configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Evaluation evidence must include: Zone and Conduit design architecture diagram, Security Level Target (SL-T) vs Security Level Achieved (SL-A) matrix, and OEM device integration manual validation registers.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
General IT-OT convergence increases the threat landscape by bridging air-gapped industrial facilities with internet-facing corporate systems. Failing to enforce strict regulatory controls risks introducing severe operational vulnerabilities.

questions.description:
Check internal device logs capturing configuration changes, reboots, and administrative adjustments.

SOP:
1. Establish physical locking covers and secure enclosures around critical field device interfaces.
2. Configure hardware configuration locks and disable local diagnostic ports (USB, RS-232) to block local unauthorized adjustments.
3. Validate that device configuration changes require double-signature supervisor tokens before logical modifications are written to memory.
4. Document the Security Level achieved (SL-A) vs target requirements (SL-T) for each zone conduit.

VERIFICATION CRITERIA:
Inspect the embedded device security configurations, check the verified logs, review the system settings, and check the following: Evaluation evidence must include: Zone and Conduit design architecture diagram, Security Level Target (SL-T) vs Security Level Achieved (SL-A) matrix, and OEM device integration manual validation registers.

OT/IT CONVERGENCE RISK:
Using unhardened or unpatched field controllers opens critical hardware interfaces to remote execution exploits. Attackers can leverage known vulnerabilities to flash unauthorized firmware or change safety threshold parameters on active PLCs.

questions.description:

SOP:
